faq.title: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about using Learn It
faq.a1: Learn It is a personal skill-learning tracker. It helps you organize learning material into structured Learning Paths, track your progress, and test your knowledge with quizzes.
faq.q2: How do I follow a course?
faq.a2: Go to "Learning Paths" and click the "Follow" button on a course card. Once followed, the course appears in "My courses" where you can track your progress. You need to follow a course before you can open it.
faq.q3: How do I import a Learning Path?
faq.a3: Click the "Import" button on the Learning Paths page, then drag & drop a .md file or click to browse. You'll see a preview of the parsed content before confirming the import. The file must be a valid .md file under 50KB.
faq.q4: How do I create my own Learning Path?
faq.a4: Create a Markdown (.md) file with YAML metadata blocks separated by ---. The first block defines global metadata (title, description, tags, difficulty, author, language). Each subsequent block defines a section with a type (concept, exercise, quiz, reference). See the full format specification in the project documentation.
faq.a5: Four types: "concept" for reading material, "exercise" for practical activities, "quiz" for knowledge checks (single or multiple choice), and "reference" for links and resources.
faq.q6: How do I create a quiz section?
faq.a6:
Set the section type to "quiz" and add a variant ("single" or "multiple"). Write each question as a ### heading, followed by options as a list. Mark the correct answer with [x] and wrong answers with [ ]. Example:

### Is useState synchronous?
- [ ] True
- [x] False
faq.q7: What happens to my progress if I unfollow a course?
faq.a7: When you unfollow a course, your progress is reset to 0%. You can always follow the course again later.
faq.a8: All data is stored locally in your browser's localStorage. Nothing is sent to any server. Your courses and progress persist across sessions until you clear your browser data.
faq.q9: Where can I find an example Learning Path?
faq.a9: You can download a sample .md file to use as a template for creating your own Learning Paths:
